construct: ROOT CAUSE OF FINANCIAL SERVICE FAILURE

Hidden Fees.definition: Customer reports unexpected, undisclosed, duplicate, or confusing charges
examples: They charged me a maintenance fee I never agreed to
I was billed twice for the same transaction

Fraud / Security Issue.definition: Unauthorized transactions, identity theft, or account compromise
include: Someone used my card without permission
exclude: Incorrect reporting without fraud (use Credit Reporting)
Fraud / Security Issue.special_rule: Fraud overrides all other labels. If fraud leads to credit reporting issue, still label Fraud
examples: There were charges from a state I've never been to
My login was changed and money disappeared
Fraud / Security Issue.business_impact: Financial loss, regulatory exposure, trust erosion

Credit Reporting Error.definition: Incorrect information sent to credit bureaus
Credit Reporting Error.clarification: If main complaint is incorrect data reported → Credit Reporting. If complaint is about dispute handling → Process Failure
examples: They reported a late payment I never missed
My credit score dropped because of wrong information

Loan/Mortgage Servicing Issue.definition: Mortgage or loan payment application issues
include: They say I missed a payment I made
examples: They say I missed a payment I clearly made

Account Access / Administration.definition: Locked accounts, account closure/freezes, login issues, inability to access funds (non-fraud)
include: Funds inaccessible due to administrative action
exclude: Service complaints about wait times (use Process Failure)
Account Access / Administration.clarification: Account locked due to suspected fraud → label as Fraud, not Account Access
examples: My account was frozen and I cannot access my money
They closed my account without warning

Process Failure / Red Tape.definition: Repeated transfers, delays, poor dispute handling, unresolved loops, lack of response
include: Called multiple times with no resolution
Process Failure / Red Tape.clarification: If customer complains mainly about poor service handling → Process Failure. If complaint centers on financial mistake → use that category instead
examples: Every time I call they tell me to call another department
They said they would call back and never did

Other.definition: Complaint does not clearly match any operational failure category
use_only_if: Ambiguous complaint without identifiable root cause

decision_rules.primary_harm_rule: Label based on operational failure that directly caused financial or access problem, not downstream consequences
decision_rules.fraud_override: If unauthorized activity mentioned, always label Fraud even if other issues follow
decision_rules.multi_issue_handling: Choose most severe operational risk. If unclear, use precedence hierarchy

labeling_principles: Do NOT label based on emotional tone
Do NOT label based on severity
Do NOT label based on number of issues mentioned
Do NOT label based on dispute history
DO label based on: What operational failure directly caused the problem?
